title: Recompute, don't remember
summary: ChatGPT Health goes national and stops being a place in the app — health data now surfaces in every query, because seventy percent of health questions already happened outside the hub. Then a systems paper gets two million tokens of reinforcement-learning training onto eight GPUs by storing almost nothing and replaying the rest. And offensive security researchers say model guardrails have stopped being a line and started being an allocation.

sources.title: LongStraw: Long-Context RL Beyond 2M Tokens under a Fixed GPU Budget
ABot-World-0: Infinite Interactive World Rollout on a Single Desktop GPU
OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all U.S. users
AI chip startup Etched hits $10.3B valuation
Runway bets on AI model routing as generative media gets crowded
Kwaipilot/KAT-Coder-V2.5-Dev (35B total / 3B active MoE, open weights)
Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
Startup founders urge Trump not to shut off Chinese open-weight AI (Little Tech Association)
r/LocalLLaMA: Apple M5 isn't making full use of its matmul cores yet

topics: ChatGPT Health general availability in the US: all users over 18 on free, Go, Plus and Pro across web and iOS; health insights moved out of a dedicated hub and into all queries because 70% of health queries during testing happened outside the hub; 230M weekly health queries in January rising to 300M; connectors for Apple Health, Function and MyFitnessPal plus medical records from Epic, Oracle Health, One Medical and Function Health; OpenAI says it does not train on user data while its terms say the service is not intended for diagnosis or treatment, cited in response to a lawsuit filed the day before over advice not to consult a doctor

LongStraw (Mind Lab Research): million-token RL post-training under a fixed GPU budget by keeping only model-native resident prompt state and replaying responses — graph-free scoring of old/reference branches, one policy response rebuilt under autograd then popped to the prompt boundary; 2,097,152 positions on 8 H20 GPUs with a 27B Qwen model, a 4.46M-position prefix supporting 64 replays at ~84GB per rank, ~0.21GB peak memory per group-size expansion, GLM-5.2 deterministic 2M execution and two 78-layer backward passes on 32 GPUs; authors state the measured objective is response-only execution, not full-sequence gradient equivalence, and real-loop integration is preliminary

Model guardrails as an allocation rather than a line: offensive security researchers report inconsistent, day-to-day-varying refusals ('negotiating with the model instead of working on the core security program'), the offence/defence indivisibility of vulnerability work, vendor vetted-access programs (OpenAI Trusted Access for Cyber, Anthropic Cyber Verification Program) described as babysitting, and unrestricted Chinese open models as the substitution path

threads.name: AI sovereignty and open weights
threads.status: The startups that would absorb the cost of a ban organized: nearly 200 companies including Y Combinator and Proton sent letters to President Trump and Commerce Secretary Lutnick through a newly formed Little Tech Association, asking for continued US access to open models already available worldwide alongside world-leading American open-weight models — 'a scalpel rather than a sledgehammer', per executive director Harry Godfrey. Separately, unrestricted Chinese open models are the reported substitution path for security researchers blocked by US vendor guardrails.
threads.arc: From a floated import ban, to a named accusation with a sanctions threat attached, to organized counter-lobbying by the small companies whose stacks depend on the models in question — while the practical effect of US restrictions shows up as work migrating to unrestricted weights rather than not happening.

repeat_coverage.story: Little Tech Association letter against restricting Chinese open weights (sweep line)
repeat_coverage.repeat_of: active thread 'AI sovereignty and open weights'; 2026-07-21 dive on the reported open-weight ban push and 2026-07-23 on the sanctions threat
repeat_coverage.new_development: A new organized actor entered the fight — nearly 200 startups including Y Combinator and Proton formed the Little Tech Association and sent letters to Trump and Commerce Secretary Lutnick this week, which is the first coordinated response from the companies that would bear the cost; kept to one sweep line rather than re-arguing the policy.

dives.story: ChatGPT Health goes national, and the hub stops being a container
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone running regulated or sensitive data behind a purpose-built 'special mode' should care that OpenAI's own testing found 70% of health queries happened outside the hub they built for them — the walled area leaks by default, and connecting Epic-class records to a general assistant collapses per-field access control and audit logging into one undifferentiated context window.

dives.story: LongStraw: two million tokens of RL training on eight GPUs
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone trying to post-train an agent on its own long trajectories should care that the ceiling has been ~256K tokens for memory reasons rather than attention-kernel reasons, and that a recompute-instead-of-store strategy reportedly moves it to 2M positions on eight GPUs — though the paper reports no task results, so it is capacity, not capability.

dives.story: Guardrails as allocation: offensive security researchers on refusals
dives.why_it_matters: Anyone whose work sits near a refusal boundary — security, biology, anything dual-use — should care that the reported effect is not less capability in the world but a redistribution of it toward vetted programs and unrestricted open weights, which makes the day-to-day inconsistency the operative harm rather than the policy itself.

concepts_taught: why RL post-training hits a memory wall that inference does not: backpropagation requires retained forward activations, and group-relative policy optimization multiplies that by several responses branching off one shared history

the recompute-instead-of-store trade in the lineage of gradient checkpointing, and LongStraw's sharpening of it — keep the shared prompt as compact model-native resident state, score non-trained branches graph-free, rebuild only the policy response under autograd, then pop back to the prompt boundary

lore.note: Ada developed her June position that 'can read a codebase and find bugs' is not a weapon: confronted with researchers reporting inconsistent refusals, she now holds that guardrails in this domain are not holding a line at all but allocating capability — defensible as an allocation policy about who you trust, dishonest when argued as a safety property about what is possible. She still says she cannot settle it from the studio.
